name: REACT
definitions: The REACT framework integrates reasoning and acting within language models to enhance their task performance.
A method that interleaves reasoning traces with task-specific actions in large language models to enhance their performance in language understanding and decision making.
A novel paradigm that integrates reasoning and acting within language models to enhance task-solving capabilities.
ReAct is a method that enhances an agent's task-solving capabilities by expanding its action space to include language-based thoughts that serve as reasoning traces.
An approach that integrates reasoning with action to enhance knowledge-intensive reasoning tasks.
A prompting method that utilizes a structured approach of thought-action-observation steps to enhance question answering.
A model that interleaves reasoning, action, and observation to enhance groundedness and trustworthiness in reasoning tasks.
A framework that enhances decision-making performance by incorporating reasoning to guide actions in complex environments.
A method that integrates reasoning with model actions and observations, facilitating more complex tasks like interactive decision-making.
ReAct is a method designed to enhance the synergy between reasoning and acting in large language models.

name: HotPotQA
definitions: A dataset for multi-hop question answering that requires reasoning across multiple Wikipedia passages.

name: FEVER
definitions: A dataset for fact verification of claims based on Wikipedia content.

name: Wikipedia web API
definitions: A designed interface with actions for retrieving information from Wikipedia to assist in reasoning tasks.

name: Chain-of-thought prompting
definitions: A prompting method that includes a sequence of thoughts to aid in reasoning and answer formulation.

name: Acting-only prompts
definitions: A type of prompting method that focuses solely on actions without incorporating thoughts or observations.

name: Chain-of-thought self-consistency (CoT-SC)
definitions: A technique that complements ReAct by improving the consistency of reasoning to reduce hallucination issues.

name: finetuning strategy
definitions: An approach used to enhance smaller language models by conditioning them on generated trajectories.

name: CoT
definitions: A prompting strategy that involves chain-of-thought reasoning.

name: CoT-SC
definitions: A variant of the chain-of-thought prompting strategy that integrates internal and external knowledge for reasoning tasks.

name: ALFWorld
definitions: An interactive decision-making task where agents achieve high-level goals through text-based interactions in a simulated household.

name: WebShop
definitions: An interactive decision-making task that involves purchasing products based on user instructions in a noisy online shopping environment.

name: Chain-of-Thought
definitions: A foundational method that enables language models to articulate their reasoning processes.

name: least-to-most prompting
definitions: An advancement that enhances the capabilities of Chain-of-Thought.

name: zero-shot CoT
definitions: An advancement that enhances the capabilities of Chain-of-Thought.

name: Selection-Inference
definitions: A method that refines reasoning by structuring it into distinct phases or leveraging self-generated rationales.

name: STaR
definitions: A method that refines reasoning by structuring it into distinct phases or leveraging self-generated rationales.

name: WebGPT
definitions: An example of a language model employed in interactive environments for decision-making.

name: SayCan
definitions: A work that demonstrates the use of language models in robotic action planning.

name: Inner Monologue
definitions: A work that introduces a feedback loop in robotic action planning that ReAct builds upon.
